steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Views increase by 60% every 2 days, starting at 233. A 60% increase means multiplying by 1.60 (not 0.60). The exponent must count how many 2-day periods fit in x days, which is x/2 (not 2x).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$f(x)=233(0.60)^{x/2}$': Base 0.60 means a 40% decrease each period — the opposite of growth. ✗
  • Choice B — '$f(x)=233(0.60)^{2x}$': Wrong base (decay) and wrong exponent (2x doubles the periods). ✗
  • Choice C — '$f(x)=233(1.60)^{x/2}$': Base 1.60 gives a 60% increase per period; exponent x/2 counts 2-day intervals correctly. ✓
  • Choice D — '$f(x)=233(1.60)^{2x}$': Correct base but exponent 2x makes the period ½ a day instead of 2 days — grows far too fast. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
